learning_outcomes.description: Develop knowledge on a high introductory level of Korean grammar, structures, and vocabulary.
Write guided short essays with familiar topics using a high introductory-level Korean grammar, vocabulary, and sentence structures.
Read, comprehend and understand a high introductory level of written texts in Korean and cultural perspectives of Korea.
Develop a high introductory level of understanding of the different aspects of the Korean culture within a global context embedded in the written form of the Korean language.
Expand the knowledge and resilience acquired in class to communicate effectively across cultures and to develop intercultural communication skills in a literacy aspect.

assessments.due_date:
28/08/2026 5:00 pm

16/10/2026 5:00 pm

Note:

  • Written Task 1 will open at 8 am on Thursday and close at 5 pm on Friday in WEEK 5.
  • Written Task 2 will open at 8 am on Thursday and close at 5 pm on Friday in WEEK 11. Once you begin Task 2, you must complete and submit your essay within 2 hours.
assessments.task_description:
General information

  • Students are required to upload their written work for the assigned tasks to Blackboard twice during the semester (Weeks 5 and 11).
  • Preparation guidelines and marking criteria for each task will be available on Blackboard.

Task 1 (Week 5)

  • Write 10 individual sentences using the given verbs.
  • Each sentence must include one conjunction (e.g. -지만, -고, -아서/해서/어서) and one noun-modifying form.
  • Write sentences 1–7 using the polite ending form (-요 form) and sentences 8–10 using the deferential form.
  • Each sentence must contain at least 8 Korean words (e.g. the sentence "오늘 / 친구하고 / 학교에 / 왔어요" consists of 4 words).
  • The task must be submitted via Blackboard.

Task 2 (Week 11)

  • This task requires students to write an essay.
  • The essay must be at least 100 Korean words in length.
  • The task must be submitted via Blackboard.

Guidelines for Using Generative AI in Assessments

  • The assignment coversheet must be submitted as part of this assessment, whether generative AI tools (e.g. ChatGPT, Google Gemini) have been used or not.
  • A coversheet template will be provided.
  • If generative AI tools are used in the assessment, students must submit both the original draft and the AI-assisted revised version. Both the original draft and the revised version must be written in Korean.
  • Any use of generative AI must be acknowledged in the assessment submission. Details of how generative AI has been used are indicated on the assignment coversheet template.
  • For further details and an example coversheet, please refer to this link: https://guides.library.uq.edu.au/referencing/generative-ai-tools-assignments.

Statement on Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

  • This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
18/09/2026 -

Note:

  • The exam will be held in the 2-hour class in WEEK 8.
assessments.task_description:
General information

The written exam will cover up to Week 7's content.

The written exam may include the following tasks:

  • Reading comprehension
  • Dialogue completion according to the given instruction
  • Completion of short dialogues
  • Translation task
  • Writing essays

Preparation guidelines and marking criteria will be available on Blackboard.

No extra time will be allowed for late students.

Statement on AI & MT

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

7/11/2026 - 21/11/2026

Note:

  • The exact examination date and time will be centrally scheduled and announced by the University closer to the examination period.
assessments.task_description:
General information

The two-hour exam will be scheduled during the regular exam period.

The goal of the End-of-semester exam is to test students' overall understanding of the Korean language skills (especially writing and reading) taught in this course.

The End-of-semester exam may include the following tasks:

  • Completion of dialogue
  • Writing an essay
  • Filling in the blanks
  • Completion of short conversations  
  • Answering the questions given in Korean

Preparation guidelines and marking criteria will be available on Blackboard.

Statement on AI & MT

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

learning_activities.topic:
Lesson 8 and 9a

Each week includes a 1-hour tutorial and a 2-hour tutorial.

  • Seeking agreement: -지요?
  • Demonstrative expression: 이/그/저
  • Deferential styles: -(스)ㅂ니다/(스)ㅂ니까?
  • N(으)로: 'toward N'
  • Irregular predicates in ㄹ
  • Expressing goal/sources: N한테/께, N 한테서
  • The noun-modifying form: Adjective stem-(으)ㄴ + N

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Lesson 8 and 9a Each week includes a 1-hour tutorial and a 2-hour tutorial. Seeking agreement: -지요? Demonstrative expression: 이/그/저 Deferential styles: -(스)ㅂ니다/(스)ㅂ니까? N(으)로: 'toward N' Irregular predicates in ㄹ Expressing goal/sources: N한테/께, N 한테서 The noun-modifying form: Adjective stem-(으)ㄴ + N

learning_activities.topic:
Lesson 9b and 10

Each week includes a 1-hour tutorial and a 2-hour tutorial.

  • Honorific expressions, the subject honorific -(으)시
  • Clausal connective: -지만
  • The clausal connective: -(으)ㄴ데/는데
  • Expressing desire: -고 싶다, -고 싶어 하다
  • The sentence ending: -(으)ㄴ데요/는데요
  • The clausal connective: -아서/해서/어서
  • The noun-modifying form: Verb stem-는 + N

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Lesson 9b and 10 Each week includes a 1-hour tutorial and a 2-hour tutorial. Honorific expressions, the subject honorific -(으)시 Clausal connective: -지만 The clausal connective: -(으)ㄴ데/는데 Expressing desire: -고 싶다, -고 싶어 하다 The sentence ending: -(으)ㄴ데요/는데요 The clausal connective: -아서/해서/어서 The noun-modifying form: Verb stem-는 + N

learning_activities.topic:
Lesson 11 and 12a

Each week includes a 1-hour tutorial and a 2-hour tutorial.

  • The progressive form: -고 있다
  • Intentional -(으)ㄹ래요?
  • N(이)나 vs N밖에
  • Asking someone's opinion: -(으)ㄹ까요?
  • The clausal connective (sequential): -아서/해서/어서
  • Conjectural -겠-

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Lesson 11 and 12a Each week includes a 1-hour tutorial and a 2-hour tutorial. The progressive form: -고 있다 Intentional -(으)ㄹ래요? N(이)나 vs N밖에 Asking someone's opinion: -(으)ㄹ까요? The clausal connective (sequential): -아서/해서/어서 Conjectural -겠-

learning_activities.topic:
Lesson 12b, 13 and revision

Each week includes a 1-hour tutorial and a 2-hour tutorial.

  • Sentence ending -네요
  • Irregular predicates in ㅎ
  • Noun modifying form: Verb stem-(으)ㄴ + N
  • The benefactive expression: -아/해/어 주다
  • Expressing obligation or necessity: -아야/해야/어야 되다
  • The sentence ending -(으)ㄹ게요
  • Noun + 때문에
  • Intentional -겠-
  • Revision

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
